findings.description: The header section with NCDENR logo, Governor name, Director name, and Secretary name loses its three-column layout in the HTML version
findings.evidence: PDF shows a three-column header with Beverly Eaves Perdue Governor on left, Charles Wakild P.E. Director in center, and Dee Freeman Secretary on right. HTML renders these as separate lines stacked vertically.

findings.description: NCDENR logo at top of page is not present in the HTML version
findings.evidence: PDF has the NCDENR logo/seal at the top center of the page. HTML does not display it.

findings.description: North Carolina Naturally logo is not present in the HTML version
findings.evidence: PDF shows the One NorthCarolina Naturally logo at the bottom right. HTML renders it as plain text.

findings.description: Horizontal rule separator line after RE section is present in PDF but not clearly preserved in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF has a clear horizontal divider line between the memo header (TO/FROM/RE) and the body text. HTML does not show this separator.

findings.description: The Corps'/DWQ table is rendered differently in HTML - as a proper HTML table with borders versus the PDF's simpler aligned columns
findings.evidence: PDF shows aligned text columns with underlined headers. HTML shows a bordered table. Data content is the same.

findings.description: The indented bullet point structure under section C is rendered as flat bullet list rather than indented sub-sections
findings.evidence: PDF shows indented bullet points under section C with the arrow bullet character. HTML uses standard bullet points.

findings.description: Bullet characters differ - PDF uses arrow (►) style bullets while HTML uses standard round bullets
findings.evidence: PDF shows ► style bullets throughout the conditions list. HTML shows standard • bullets.

findings.description: Italic formatting for italic text blocks in the culvert conditions section is preserved but styled slightly differently
findings.evidence: PDF shows italic text for condition requirements. HTML preserves the italic formatting.

findings.description: The bracketed editorial notes in the PDF use regular text, which matches the HTML treatment
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML show the bracketed notes [Condition has been updated...] in regular text

findings.description: The hierarchical bullet structure with sub-items under Compensatory Mitigation is flattened slightly in HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows clear hierarchical indentation under the Compensatory Mitigation section with ► bullets. HTML uses standard list formatting.

findings.description: Sub-list items a through i under section E are rendered as part of running text rather than as a distinct lettered list in the HTML
findings.evidence: PDF shows items a. through i. as clearly indented lettered items. HTML renders them in running paragraph form with letter prefixes.

findings.description: The cc section at the bottom maintains the same content but loses the tabular alignment
findings.evidence: PDF shows cc: with names listed below in an indented block. HTML shows them in a single line.

findings.description: No h1 heading exists in the HTML document. The document lacks any heading hierarchy whatsoever - all section titles are rendered as bold paragraph text or list items rather than proper heading elements (h1-h6).
findings.evidence: The PDF has clear section headings like 'I. Umbrella Mitigation Banking Instrument (UMBI) Template for Nutrient Offset Mitigation Banks' and 'II. Proposed Water Quality Certification...' plus lettered subsections A through E. The HTML uses <strong> tags and <ul>/<li> elements instead of heading tags.

findings.description: Each page of the PDF is represented twice in the HTML - once as an embedded image (with alt text describing it as a page image) and once as converted text content. This creates duplication of all content across all 7 pages.
findings.evidence: For example, page 1 appears as '' followed by the full text of page 1. This pattern repeats for all 7 pages. The images are full-page scans that duplicate the text content.

findings.description: The PDF text on page 3 reads 'All applications shall address stormwater management' (note: 'applications' not 'applicationshall'). The source PDF itself appears to have 'All applications shall' though the OCR text shows 'applicationshall' run together. The HTML correctly renders it as 'All applications shall'.
findings.evidence: PDF page 3: 'All applicationshall address stormwater management' vs HTML: 'All applications shall address stormwater management' - this is actually a correction of what appears to be an OCR error in the source, which is acceptable.

findings.description: On page 4, the PDF text reads 'restored to original grade' but one instance shows 'oil and grade' in the PDF OCR text. The HTML correctly renders 'original grade'.
findings.evidence: PDF page 4 OCR: 'restored to oil§nd grade' vs HTML: 'restored to original grade' - this appears to be a correct interpretation of the actual PDF text.

findings.description: URLs from the PDF are present in the HTML as plain text rather than as clickable hyperlinks in most cases. The PDF contains URLs that are referenced but the HTML does not consistently wrap them in anchor tags.
findings.evidence: URLs like 'http://portal.ncdenr.org/web/wq/swp/ws/401/publicnotices' appear as plain text in paragraphs rather than as <a> hyperlinks.

findings.description: Alt text for embedded page images is generic rather than descriptive of actual content.
findings.evidence: Alt texts like 'Page 1 of the NCDENR memorandum', 'Page 2 of the NCDENR memorandum' etc. are used for what appear to be full-page document scans. While not fabricated, they are minimally descriptive.
